name: Energy Plus Credit Union
description_short: Energy Plus Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution offering savings accounts, checking, loans, mortgages, and digital banking services with competitive rates and member benefits.
description_long:
Energy Plus Credit Union (EPCU) is a credit union that provides comprehensive financial services to its members through both in-person and digital channels. The organization operates as a member-owned cooperative financial institution, distinguishing it from traditional banks by returning profits to members rather than shareholders. According to their website, EPCU recently underwent a merger, as indicated by their FAQ-Merger resource section, suggesting organizational growth and consolidation within the credit union sector.

EPCU offers a full suite of consumer financial products including savings accounts, checking accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), auto loans, RV/camper loans, home equity lines of credit, and first mortgages. Their current advertised rates include auto loans as low as 4.25% APR, RV loans at 7.25% APR, home equity lines starting at 6.75% APR, and certificate accounts up to 4.00% APY. The credit union provides digital banking through online platforms, mobile apps for iOS and Android, and mobile deposit capabilities, enabling members to manage finances remotely with 24/7 access.

EPCU distinguishes itself through several member-centric features and partnerships. The organization offers a Folds of Honor debit card where every transaction supports military families, demonstrating community engagement. They provide a member referral program offering $25 to both referrer and new member, plus entry into a $500 drawing. EPCU partners with Minnesota Life for credit life and disability insurance, Trustage for accident coverage, and J.D. Power for car pricing information. The credit union also maintains a fraud protection hub and offers educational resources like financial calculators and a fraud alert system.

As a credit union, EPCU provides member-focused benefits typically associated with cooperative financial institutions, including potentially lower fees and competitive rates. However, membership requirements and eligibility criteria are not detailed on the homepage, and specific fee structures require accessing their fee schedule document. The organization appears to be mid-sized based on service offerings and recently navigated a merger, which may indicate ongoing integration or changes to service delivery. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Competitive auto loan rates starting at 4.25% APR with promotional 1.00% APR discount option
No-payment-for-90-days option on auto loans providing payment flexibility
24/7 online and mobile banking with mobile deposit capability
Member referral program offering $25 bonus plus $500 drawing entry
Folds of Honor debit card directing merchant fees to military family support
Partnership with Minnesota Life for credit life and disability insurance coverage
Free mobile apps for iOS and Android with full account management features
cons: Membership eligibility requirements not clearly stated on homepage, requiring additional research
Home equity line of credit rates starting at 6.75% APR are higher than some competing lenders
Limited product transparency—specific fees and account minimums require accessing separate documents
Recent merger (FAQ-Merger section) may indicate ongoing service integration or potential changes
No mention of checking account interest rates or specific account comparison tools
best_for: Military families seeking to support their community through debit card transactions
Credit union members eligible to join seeking competitive auto and RV financing
Members wanting digital-first banking with 24/7 online and mobile access
Homebuyers interested in mortgage services with dedicated mortgage assistance team
diagnosis: Energy Plus Credit Union is best for members seeking a full-service financial institution with competitive rates, digital banking convenience, and member-owned cooperative benefits. The main caveat is that specific membership eligibility requirements, detailed fee structures, and account minimums are not transparent on the homepage and require additional investigation before joining.
services: RV and camper loans starting at 7.25% APR
Home equity lines of credit from 6.75% APR
First mortgage services for home purchase
Online banking platform with bill pay
Mobile banking apps for iOS and Android
Credit life and disability insurance through Minnesota Life
Folds of Honor military support debit card
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
